(P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県秩父市下吉田7377', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11540486}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=10 July 2019 (Wed) 03:29 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Muku Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}, {{jalink|Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Chichibu District, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, listed in the "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{jalink|Chichibu District|Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{jalink|Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207}}, where {{jalink|Ryusei rockets|Q11679206}} are launched during the {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{jalink|Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{jalink|Kusa no Ran|Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hime-no-Mikoto}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{jalink|710|Q23518}} ({{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3), when {{jalink|Tajihi no Mahito}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{jalink|Chichibu Station|Q5096049}} on {{jalink|Chichibu Railway|Q843780}} or {{jalink|Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649}}, take {{jalink|Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県秩父市下吉田7377', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11540486}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=10 July 2019 (Wed) 03:29 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Muku Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}, {{jalink|Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Chichibu District, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, listed in the "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{jalink|Chichibu District|Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{jalink|Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207}}, where {{jalink|Ryusei rockets|Q11679206}} are launched during the {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{jalink|Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{jalink|Kusa no Ran|Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hime-no-Mikoto}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{jalink|710|Q23518}} ({{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3), when {{jalink|Tajihi no Mahito}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{jalink|Chichibu Station|Q5096049}} on {{jalink|Chichibu Railway|Q843780}} or {{jalink|Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649}}, take {{jalink|Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{jalink|Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207}}, where {{jalink|Ryusei rockets|Q11679206}} are launched during the {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}, is well known. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{jalink|Kusa no Ran|Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hime-no-Mikoto}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{jalink|710|Q23518}} ({{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3), when {{jalink|Tajihi no Mahito}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{jalink|Chichibu Station|Q5096049}} on {{jalink|Chichibu Railway|Q843780}} or {{jalink|Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649}}, take {{jalink|Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}, {{jalink|Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Chichibu District, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, listed in the "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{jalink|Chichibu District|Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{jalink|Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207}}, where {{jalink|Ryusei rockets|Q11679206}} are launched during the {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{jalink|Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{jalink|Kusa no Ran|Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hime-no-Mikoto}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{jalink|710|Q23518}} ({{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3), when {{jalink|Tajihi no Mahito}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{jalink|Chichibu Station|Q5096049}} on {{jalink|Chichibu Railway|Q843780}} or {{jalink|Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649}}, take {{jalink|Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県秩父市下吉田7377', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11540486}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=10 July 2019 (Wed) 03:29 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Muku Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}, {{jalink|Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Chichibu District, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, listed in the "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{jalink|Chichibu District|Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{jalink|Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207}}, where {{jalink|Ryusei rockets|Q11679206}} are launched during the {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{jalink|Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{jalink|Kusa no Ran|Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hime-no-Mikoto}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{jalink|710|Q23518}} ({{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3), when {{jalink|Tajihi no Mahito}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{jalink|Chichibu Station|Q5096049}} on {{jalink|Chichibu Railway|Q843780}} or {{jalink|Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649}}, take {{jalink|Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}, {{jalink|Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Chichibu District, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, listed in the "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Chichibu|Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{jalink|Chichibu District|Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{jalink|Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207}}, where {{jalink|Ryusei rockets|Q11679206}} are launched during the {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{jalink|Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{jalink|Kusa no Ran|Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hime-no-Mikoto}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{jalink|710|Q23518}} ({{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3), when {{jalink|Tajihi no Mahito}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{jalink|Chichibu Station|Q5096049}} on {{jalink|Chichibu Railway|Q843780}} or {{jalink|Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649}}, take {{jalink|Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県秩父市下吉田7377', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11540486}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=10 July 2019 (Wed) 03:29 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Muku Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of Chichibu District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, listed in the "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{ill|lt=Chichibu District|Q1145581|WD=Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{ill|lt=Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207|WD=Q11679207}}, where {{ill|lt=Ryusei rockets|Q11679206|WD=Q11679206}} are launched during the {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{ill|lt=Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|WD=Q3078148|lt=Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{ill|lt=Kusa no Ran|Q11618062|WD=Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajihi no Mahito|UNKNOWN}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{ill|lt=Chichibu Station|Q5096049|WD=Q5096049}} on {{ill|lt=Chichibu Railway|Q843780|WD=Q843780}} or {{ill|lt=Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590|WD=Q7446590}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649|WD=Q842649}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934|WD=Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822|WD=Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Chichibu District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, listed in the "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{ill|lt=Chichibu District|Q1145581|WD=Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{ill|lt=Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207|WD=Q11679207}}, where {{ill|lt=Ryusei rockets|Q11679206|WD=Q11679206}} are launched during the {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{ill|lt=Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|WD=Q3078148|lt=Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{ill|lt=Kusa no Ran|Q11618062|WD=Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajihi no Mahito|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{ill|lt=Chichibu Station|Q5096049|WD=Q5096049}} on {{ill|lt=Chichibu Railway|Q843780|WD=Q843780}} or {{ill|lt=Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590|WD=Q7446590}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649|WD=Q842649}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934|WD=Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822|WD=Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{ill|lt=Kusa no Ran|Q11618062|WD=Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajihi no Mahito|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{ill|lt=Chichibu Station|Q5096049|WD=Q5096049}} on {{ill|lt=Chichibu Railway|Q843780|WD=Q843780}} or {{ill|lt=Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590|WD=Q7446590}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649|WD=Q842649}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934|WD=Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822|WD=Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of Chichibu District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, listed in the "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{ill|lt=Chichibu District|Q1145581|WD=Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{ill|lt=Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207|WD=Q11679207}}, where {{ill|lt=Ryusei rockets|Q11679206|WD=Q11679206}} are launched during the {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{ill|lt=Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|WD=Q3078148|lt=Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{ill|lt=Kusa no Ran|Q11618062|WD=Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajihi no Mahito|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{ill|lt=Chichibu Station|Q5096049|WD=Q5096049}} on {{ill|lt=Chichibu Railway|Q843780|WD=Q843780}} or {{ill|lt=Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590|WD=Q7446590}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649|WD=Q842649}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934|WD=Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県秩父市下吉田7377', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11540486}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=10 July 2019 (Wed) 03:29 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Muku Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of Chichibu District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, listed in the "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{ill|lt=Chichibu District|Q1145581|WD=Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{ill|lt=Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207|WD=Q11679207}}, where {{ill|lt=Ryusei rockets|Q11679206|WD=Q11679206}} are launched during the {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{ill|lt=Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|WD=Q3078148|lt=Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{ill|lt=Kusa no Ran|Q11618062|WD=Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajihi no Mahito|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{ill|lt=Chichibu Station|Q5096049|WD=Q5096049}} on {{ill|lt=Chichibu Railway|Q843780|WD=Q843780}} or {{ill|lt=Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590|WD=Q7446590}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649|WD=Q842649}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934|WD=Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822|WD=Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[ファイル:Muku-jinjya.JPG|250px|Muku Shrine]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}}Shimoyoshida 7377 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Muku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Muku Shrine''' (むくじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of Chichibu District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, listed in the "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}." There are five shrines of the same name in the Chichibu area, and it is said that the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} government allowed all of them to claim Shikinaisha status. * Muku Shrine ({{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Chichibu|Q647079|WD=Q647079}} Shimoyoshida) * Muku Shrine (Minano, {{ill|lt=Chichibu District|Q1145581|WD=Q1145581}}, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Nomaki, Minano, Chichibu District, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Muku Shrine (Makita, Chichibu, Saitama) * Gosho Shrine (Ikota, Chichibu, Saitama) ... said to be a divided spirit from Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu. This article describes Muku Shrine in Shimoyoshida, Chichibu, Saitama. === Overview === Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It was originally called Ikura Gosho Daimyojin, and "Ikura Jinja" is considered the original reading. In the early modern period, people from outside the area increasingly read it as "Muku Jinja," which became the current name. {{ill|lt=Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei|Q11679207|WD=Q11679207}}, where {{ill|lt=Ryusei rockets|Q11679206|WD=Q11679206}} are launched during the {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}, is well known. On October 31, Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}}), it was also the site of the uprising rally of the {{ill|lt=Chichibu Incident|Q3078148|WD=Q3078148|lt=Chichibu Konminto}}. In 2004, the 120th anniversary year of the Chichibu Incident, filming for the commemorative movie "{{ill|lt=Kusa no Ran|Q11618062|WD=Q11618062}}" was held here. === Enshrined deities === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto was enshrined. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *: During Yamato Takeru's eastern expedition, guided by a shining spear, Onamuchi-no-Mikoto appeared from the shade of a muku tree by a well and guided the way. The spear was treated as the sacred object and Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition. The origin of the shrine building is said to be in {{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajihi no Mahito|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated five to of rice and labor service. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Muku deity * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 13 (871), Nov 10 - Junior Fifth Rank Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Vol. 20, Jogan 13 (871), 11th month, 10th day. * {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 2 (1382) - Junior Second Rank ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Muku Shrine * Meiji 6 - Gosha * Meiji 15, June 15 - Prefectural shrine * Taisho 10, October 4 - Shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshin shrine === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrine to the left of the first torii * Ryokami Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Inari Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Shiratori Shrine Combined hall of precinct shrines on the left side of the main hall * Shinmei Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, Suwa Shrine Precinct shrine on the left side of the main hall * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine - on Nov 13, Taisho 5, the mansion shrine of Chichibu Juro Taketsuna's residence was merged. === Cultural properties === * Nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Chichibu Yoshida no Ryusei * Chichibu City designated Tangible Cultural Property ** Muku Shrine main hall ** Former main hall of Hachiman Shrine ** Suji kabuto (helmet) === Access === '''Shimoyoshida Muku Shrine''' * From {{ill|lt=Chichibu Station|Q5096049|WD=Q5096049}} on {{ill|lt=Chichibu Railway|Q843780|WD=Q843780}} or {{ill|lt=Seibu-Chichibu Station|Q7446590|WD=Q7446590}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Chichibu Line|Q842649|WD=Q842649}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Kanko Bus|Q11628934|WD=Q11628934}} bound for "Yoshida Genkimura" (Chichibu Yoshida line). Get off at "Ryusei Kaikan" and walk 10 minutes. *: Note: buses are infrequent. '''Naka-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Naka-Makita" stop. '''Kami-Makita Muku Shrine''' * From Chichibu Station or Seibu-Chichibu Station, take Seibu Kanko Bus bound for "Ogano Depot" or "Kurio." Get off at "Fusaka Iriguchi" stop. === Location === * Shimoyoshida 7377, Chichibu, Saitama :{{ウィキ座標度分秒|36|2|42.32|N|139|1|58.52|E|}} :[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?b=360242.32&l=1390158.52 GSI Map Viewer 1:25,000 map: Minano] === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nonko 36-sai (Kaji tetsudai)|Q11325822|WD=Q11325822}} (2008 Japanese film; location shooting at Muku Shrine) === External links === * [http://www1.city.chichibu.lg.jp/bunkazai/ Cultural Properties of Chichibu City - Chichibu City Board of Education] * [http://www.ryusei.biz/history/jiken02.html Introduction to the film "Kusa no Ran" - Chichibu Tourism Association Yoshida Branch] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
